products.title: Bio-Bitumen
products.subtitle: Pyrolysis-based renewable binder for sustainable roads
products.titleTag: Bio-Bitumen Plant Setup & PMC in India | YUGA
products.metaDesc: Set up a bio-bitumen plant with YUGA's end-to-end PMC — CSIR-CRRI KrishiBind spec, ~15–30% agro-waste bio-oil blend, DPR to commissioning across India.
intro: Bio-bitumen is an eco-friendly alternative to petroleum bitumen, produced by converting biomass and agricultural residues (rice straw, lignin, crop waste) into bio-oil through pyrolysis. It can partially or fully replace conventional bitumen in road construction.

India became the first country to commercially produce bio-bitumen, with CSIR-CRRI and CSIR-IIP developing the indigenous technology and the first bio-bitumen National Highway stretch laid on NH-44 near Nagpur.
benefits: Reduces import dependence — India imports ~40% of its bitumen; bio-bitumen cuts this and saves foreign exchange
Lower emissions — up to ~70% lower greenhouse-gas footprint than fossil bitumen
Circular economy — uses crop residue, helping reduce stubble burning
Comparable performance — field trials show good monsoon durability and binding strength
Government push — strongly supported by MoRTH / NHAI for green highways
applications: Bituminous wearing and binder courses on highways and rural roads
Blending (typically up to 15%) with conventional bitumen
Green / sustainable infrastructure and carbon-credit linked projects
Premix carpet, surface dressing and patch-repair mixes

details.label: Capacity to show
details.value: Pilot 5–10 TPD biomass input; Commercial 20–50 TPD

details.label: Area required
details.value: 10 TPD: 1.5–3 acres; 20–50 TPD: 4–8 acres (biomass yard, drying, pyrolysis block, blending/tank farm, lab, green belt, fire movement)

details.label: Approx. cost (without land)
details.value: 10 TPD ₹6–10 Cr; 20–50 TPD ₹15–35 Cr (depends on tech licence, upgrading, tank farm, road-testing scope)

details.label: Utilities
details.value: Power, thermic oil/heating fuel, cooling water, compressed air, fire water, biomass handling

details.label: Approvals
details.value: Technology licence, SPCB CTE/CTO, factory licence, fire NOC, thermic-fluid safety, MoRTH/IRC/BIS compliance

details.label: Best-use states
details.value: Punjab, Haryana, UP, MP, Chhattisgarh, Maharashtra (agro-residue + road demand)

machinery: Bale opener, conveyor, magnetic separator, dust collection
Biomass pyrolysis reactor with feeder & discharge
Cyclone, condenser train, bio-oil tank, gas handling

financials.note: Indicative for an emerging commercial-scale bio-bitumen / bio-oil unit.

rows.label: Project investment (CAPEX)
rows.value: ₹6 crore – ₹35 crore (10–50 TPD, integrated biomass→bio-binder; a basic bio-oil/biochar unit is lower, ~₹3–3.6 Cr at 10 TPD)

qa.a: A binder made from biomass/agro-waste bio-oil (via pyrolysis) that replaces part or all of petroleum bitumen in road mixes.

qa.q: Is it as strong as normal bitumen?
qa.a: Field pilots in India show comparable binding and monsoon durability at blended ratios; full-replacement grades are advancing through R&D.
Lignin, rice/wheat straw, sugarcane and other lignocellulosic agricultural residues.
Biomass is pyrolysed into bio-oil, which is refined and formulated into a road binder.

qa.q: Is it approved for highways in India?
qa.a: Yes — a bio-bitumen stretch is already laid on NH-44, and MoRTH/NHAI actively promote green binders.

qa.q: How much conventional bitumen can it replace?
qa.a: Commonly up to about 15% today, with higher replacement being trialled.

qa.q: Is it cheaper than imported bitumen?
qa.a: It reduces dependence on imported crude/bitumen and can lower lifecycle cost, especially where feedstock is local and cheap.
Yes — lower GHG emissions and productive use of crop residue instead of burning it.
NHAI/state PWD contractors, road developers, and infrastructure companies pursuing sustainability targets.

qa.q: Can you set up a bio-bitumen plant for me?
qa.a: Yes — as PMC we handle feasibility, technology selection, feedstock tie-up, plant setup, approvals and offtake/marketing.

products.title: Plastic-to-Fuel (Plastic Pyrolysis)
products.subtitle: Converting waste plastic into pyrolysis oil, gas and char
products.titleTag: Plastic-to-Fuel Pyrolysis Plant PMC, India | YUGA
products.metaDesc: Build a plastic pyrolysis plant turning waste plastic into fuel oil, gas & char. YUGA handles feasibility, DPR, licences & commissioning — EPR-aligned.
intro: Plastic-to-fuel uses pyrolysis — heating mixed/non-recyclable plastic waste in the absence of oxygen — to produce pyrolysis oil (a fuel/feedstock), combustible gas (reused for heating) and carbon char. It turns a waste-management problem into a revenue stream.

The business is supported by India's Plastic Waste Management Rules, Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and the single-use-plastic ban, which create strong feedstock availability and policy backing.
benefits: Solves waste — diverts non-recyclable plastic from landfill and oceans
Multiple revenue — pyrolysis oil, off-gas (self-fuel) and carbon char
Low feedstock cost — plastic waste is cheap and abundant
Policy support — EPR + SUP ban drive demand for plastic processing
Fast ROI — well-run units report payback within ~2–3 years
applications: Pyrolysis oil sold as industrial furnace/boiler fuel or refinery feedstock
Carbon char used in low-grade carbon / brick / fuel applications
Off-gas recycled to fire the reactor (lower running cost)
EPR / circular-economy compliance projects for brand owners

specs.label: Key approvals
specs.value: CPCB/SPCB consent, EPR registration, factory licence

details.label: Capacity to show
details.value: Entry 5 TPD; Common 10 TPD; Larger 20 TPD+

details.label: Area required
details.value: 10 TPD: 1–2 acres practical plot (storage, segregation, fire safety, APC clearance need space)

details.label: Approx. cost (without land)
details.value: 5 TPD ₹1.5–2.5 Cr; 10 TPD ₹3–5 Cr; 20 TPD ₹6–10 Cr (incl. APC, utilities, tanks, civil/electrical)

details.label: Utilities
details.value: Electricity, process heat/fuel, cooling water, scrubber water/chemicals, nitrogen/inerting where used, fire water, forklift/loader

details.label: Approvals
details.value: SPCB CTE/CTO, plastic-waste processor / EPR registration, factory/fire, hazardous-waste authorization if triggered, storage permission, buyer legality

details.label: Best-use states
details.value: Gujarat, Maharashtra, UP, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Telangana, Rajasthan, Delhi-NCR

machinery: Feeding system with air-lock or screw feeder
Scrubber, stack, monitoring ports, safety systems

financials.note: Indicative for a ~5–10 TPD plastic pyrolysis unit.

qa.a: A pyrolysis process that converts waste plastic into pyrolysis oil, gas and char without burning it in open air.
Mostly polyolefins — LDPE, HDPE, PP and multilayer/mixed plastic that is hard to recycle mechanically. PVC and PET are limited/avoided.
Pyrolysis (fuel) oil, combustible gas (reused to heat the reactor) and carbon char.
Industries running furnaces/boilers, and refiners/processors using it as feedstock.
Yes, with CPCB/SPCB consent and compliance with Plastic Waste Management Rules and the CPCB SOP for pyrolysis units.

qa.q: How much land and investment is needed?
qa.a: Varies by capacity; small commercial units start modest and scale. We size this precisely in the feasibility/DPR stage.
Many well-structured units target payback in about 2–3 years through oil + char + gas revenues.

qa.q: Where do I get raw material?
qa.a: Municipal waste, scrap dealers, MRFs and industrial plastic waste; we help set up feedstock supply chains.
Modern PLC-controlled, closed-loop plants with scrubbers and emission control are compliant; open burning is not permitted.

qa.q: Can you help me set it up end-to-end?
qa.a: Yes — feasibility, location, technology, machinery, approvals, commissioning and product sales are all part of our PMC scope.

products.title: Rubber-to-Fuel (Tyre Pyrolysis)
products.subtitle: Waste tyres into Tyre Pyrolysis Oil (TPO), carbon black, steel & gas
products.metaDesc: Convert end-of-life tyres into pyrolysis oil, carbon black & steel. YUGA's PMC covers DPR, licences, machinery & buyer support for tyre-to-oil plants in India.
intro: Tyre pyrolysis (rubber-to-fuel) thermally decomposes end-of-life tyres into Tyre Pyrolysis Oil (TPO), recovered carbon black (rCB), steel wire and combustible gas. India generates large volumes of waste tyres, making feedstock plentiful.

Operations must follow the CPCB SOP for TPO units (latest revision), including PLC automation, Zero Liquid Discharge and EPR registration. Newer norms favour continuous-type, higher-capacity, well-controlled plants.
benefits: Four revenue streams — TPO oil, carbon black, steel wire and gas
Abundant feedstock — millions of end-of-life tyres each year
Strong margins — payback frequently in ~12–24 months when optimised
Environmental value — removes a hazardous waste-tyre stockpile
EPR aligned — supports tyre producers' recycling obligations
applications: TPO sold as industrial fuel or further refined
Recovered carbon black for rubber, plastic, masterbatch, pigment uses
Steel wire scrap sold to steel/foundry units
Gas recycled to heat the reactor, reducing running cost

specs.value: CPCB SOP for TPO units; PLC + Zero Liquid Discharge

specs.label: Land (typical)
specs.value: Larger continuous units need sizeable plots (e.g. ~7,000 m²+)

Tyre Pyrolysis Oil, recovered carbon black, steel wire and combustible off-gas.
Industries using furnace/boiler fuel, and processors who refine it further.
Yes — recovered carbon black has resale value for rubber, plastics, paints and masterbatch after processing.
Adherence to the CPCB SOP: PLC automation, Zero Liquid Discharge, emission control, EPR registration and SPCB consent.

products.title: PMB — Polymer Modified Bitumen
products.subtitle: High-performance binder for heavy-duty roads (IS 15462:2019)
products.titleTag: PMB Plant Setup — Polymer Modified Bitumen | YUGA
products.metaDesc: Produce Polymer Modified Bitumen (IS 15462:2019) for highways & heavy traffic. YUGA delivers full PMB plant PMC — DPR, licences, machinery & market support.
intro: Polymer Modified Bitumen (PMB) is conventional bitumen modified with polymers (commonly SBS) to improve elasticity, temperature resistance, fatigue life and resistance to rutting. It is specified under IS 15462:2019.

PMB is recommended for heavy-traffic pavements — expressways, national highways, urban arterials, junctions and high-rainfall or high-temperature zones — where ordinary bitumen would deform or crack faster.
benefits: Resists rutting — holds shape under heavy loads and high heat
Resists cracking — better low-temperature and fatigue performance
Longer life — extended pavement service life and lower maintenance
Better adhesion — improved binding and water resistance
Standardised — graded as PMB 40 / PMB 70 / PMB 120 per IS 15462:2019
applications: Expressways, national & state highways with heavy commercial traffic
Stone Matrix Asphalt (SMA) and high-stress wearing courses
Junctions, toll plazas, ports, climbing lanes (braking/wheel wander)
High-rainfall and high-temperature road corridors

specs.label: Plant
specs.value: Inline/batch PMB blending unit with colloidal mill

details.value: Small 3–5 TPH; Commercial 10–20 TPH; Large 30 TPH+

details.label: Area required
details.value: 10 TPH: 20,000–40,000 sq ft; with large tank farm/loading 1–1.5 acres

details.label: Approx. cost (without land)
details.value: 5 TPH ₹1–2.5 Cr; 10 TPH ₹2.5–5 Cr; 20–30 TPH ₹5–10 Cr (depends on tank farm, automation, polymer dosing)

details.label: Utilities
details.value: Power, thermic-fluid heat, compressed air, diesel/furnace oil/gas, firefighting water, lab chemicals

details.label: Approvals
details.value: SPCB CTE/CTO, factory/fire, petroleum/storage if applicable, boiler/thermic-fluid safety, PMB-grade spec compliance

rows.label: Working capital
rows.value: High — bitumen is a costly input

qa.a: Bitumen modified with polymer (usually SBS) to make roads stronger, more elastic and longer-lasting.

qa.q: How is PMB different from normal bitumen?
qa.a: It resists rutting and cracking far better across hot and cold temperatures and heavy traffic.
IS 15462:2019 in India, with grades PMB 40, PMB 70 and PMB 120.
Expressways, national highways, SMA wearing courses, junctions, toll plazas and high-stress roads.
It depends on climate and traffic — softer grades for cold regions, stiffer grades for hot/heavy-traffic regions.

qa.q: Is PMB costlier than VG bitumen?
qa.a: Per tonne yes, but lower maintenance and longer life usually reduce lifecycle cost.
Most commonly SBS (an elastomer); plastomers are used in specific cases.
Yes, with a PMB blending plant (colloidal mill + storage); we help with plant selection and IS-compliant formulation.

qa.q: Do you set up PMB plants?

products.title: CRMB — Crumb Rubber Modified Bitumen
products.subtitle: Waste-tyre crumb rubber modified binder (IS 15462 / IS 17079)
products.titleTag: CRMB Plant — Crumb Rubber Modified Bitumen | YUGA
products.metaDesc: Set up a CRMB plant making crumb-rubber modified bitumen (IS 15462 / IS 17079) for NHAI-grade roads. YUGA covers DPR to commissioning across India.
intro: Crumb Rubber Modified Bitumen (CRMB) is bitumen modified with crumb rubber recovered from waste tyres, improving elasticity, fatigue life and temperature susceptibility while consuming a waste material. Common grades include CRMB 50, CRMB 55 and CRMB 60.
CRMB delivers much of the performance of PMB, often at lower cost, and supports the circular economy by reusing scrap tyre rubber. It is widely used on highways and heavy-traffic urban roads.
benefits: Cost-effective performance — strong results, usually cheaper than PMB
Uses waste tyres — consumes crumb rubber, an environmental plus
Better elasticity & life — improved fatigue and rut resistance
Temperature tolerant — performs across hot Indian summers
Standard grades — CRMB 50 / 55 / 60 to suit climate zones
applications: National & state highways and heavy-traffic city roads
Hot-mix wearing and binder courses
Regions prioritising waste-tyre reuse / green procurement
Overlays and strengthening of existing pavements

qa.q: How is CRMB different from PMB?
qa.a: CRMB uses recycled tyre rubber and is typically cheaper; PMB uses virgin polymer (SBS) and suits the heaviest-duty roads.
CRMB 50, CRMB 55 and CRMB 60, selected by climate and traffic.

qa.q: Is CRMB good for hot climates?
qa.a: Yes — it has good temperature susceptibility and performs well in hot regions.

qa.q: Can it be made on-site or at a plant?
qa.a: Both — via dedicated CRMB blending plants; we advise on the right configuration.

products.title: Bitumen Decanter
products.subtitle: Drum decanting / packaging system for safe bitumen handling
products.titleTag: Bitumen Decanter & Packaging Plant PMC | YUGA
products.metaDesc: Safe drum decanting & packaging systems for bitumen handling. YUGA's PMC plans, sources & commissions bitumen decanter units with full compliance support.
intro: A bitumen decanter is a system that safely melts and discharges bitumen from drums (or processes bulk bitumen) using controlled heating, so it can be transferred to storage tanks, tankers or packaging without manual breaking of drums.

Decanting improves safety, reduces wastage and labour, and supports value-added packaging (e.g. converting bulk/imported bitumen into drums, bags or modified products). It is a key utility for terminals, emulsion and PMB/CRMB plants.
benefits: Safe handling — controlled heating avoids hazardous manual drum cutting
Less wastage — clean, near-complete recovery of bitumen
Lower labour — mechanised, faster decanting
Feeds value addition — supplies tanks for emulsion / PMB / CRMB / packing
Flexible — handles drums or bulk depending on configuration
applications: Bitumen terminals and storage depots
Emulsion, PMB and CRMB manufacturing plants (feed system)
Drum / bag / small-pack repackaging units
Contractors needing reliable molten bitumen supply

specs.label: Function
specs.value: Melt & discharge bitumen from drums / bulk

specs.label: Typical add-ons
specs.value: Storage tanks, pumps, weighing, filling station

specs.label: Throughput
specs.value: Sized to plant demand (drums per hour)

details.value: Small 2–4 TPH; Common 6–10 TPH; Large/terminal 12 TPH+

details.label: Area required
details.value: Standalone 4–10 TPH: 5,000–15,000 sq ft; with tank farm & loading 20,000–40,000 sq ft

details.label: Approvals

details.label: Best-use note
details.value: Usually supports emulsion/PMB/CRMB/hot-mix/road-site ops rather than a standalone high-margin business

machinery: Drum/bag loading platform or hydraulic loading

financials.note: A decanter is a utility/value-addition system, not a standalone product; figures are equipment-based.

rows.label: Role
rows.value: Feeds emulsion / PMB / CRMB / packaging lines

qa.a: A heated system that melts and discharges bitumen from drums or bulk so it can be pumped into tanks, tankers or packaging.
To handle bitumen safely, avoid cutting drums by hand, reduce wastage, and feed downstream plants.
Usually by indirect thermic-fluid (hot-oil) coils for safe, controlled melting without direct flame on product.

qa.q: What can I do after decanting?
qa.a: Store it, load tankers, repack into drums/bags, or feed emulsion/PMB/CRMB production.

qa.q: Is it safer than manual handling?
qa.a: Yes — it greatly reduces fire risk, burns and product loss compared with manual drum breaking.
Terminals, depots, packers, contractors and any modified-bitumen or emulsion plant.

qa.q: Can it handle both drums and bulk?
qa.a: Yes, depending on the configuration we specify for your operation.
It is sized to your throughput (drums/tonnes per hour); we calculate this in the feasibility/DPR.

qa.q: Does it integrate with other plants?
qa.a: Yes — it commonly acts as the feed/storage front-end for emulsion, PMB and CRMB units.

qa.q: Can you supply and install it?
qa.a: Yes — we handle selection, layout, supply, installation and integration as part of our PMC scope.

products.title: Bitumen Emulsion
products.subtitle: Water-based bitumen for cold-application road works (IS 8887)
products.titleTag: Bitumen Emulsion Plant Setup in India | YUGA
products.metaDesc: Manufacture water-based bitumen emulsion (IS 8887) for cold-mix & tack coats. YUGA's PMC takes your emulsion plant from DPR and licences to production.
intro: Bitumen emulsion is a stable mix of bitumen droplets dispersed in water with an emulsifier, allowing bitumen to be used cold (without high-temperature heating). It is graded as Rapid (RS), Medium (MS) and Slow (SS) setting, and specified under IS 8887.

Emulsions are widely used for tack coats, prime coats, surface dressing, fog seals, patch repair and cold-mix works — making them ideal for maintenance, rural roads and eco-friendly, low-energy applications.
benefits: Cold application — no heating to high temperature — safer, less fuel
Eco-friendly — water-based, lower emissions and energy use
Versatile — tack, prime, surface dressing, fog seal, cold mix, repair
Good for maintenance — ideal for patchwork and rural roads
Standardised — RS / MS / SS grades per IS 8887
applications: Tack coat and prime coat between/under pavement layers
Surface dressing and fog seal
Pothole / patch repair and cold-mix maintenance
Rural roads (PMGSY-type works) and all-weather repair

qa.a: Bitumen dispersed in water with an emulsifier, so it can be applied cold without high-temperature heating.

qa.q: What are RS, MS and SS grades?
qa.a: Rapid-Setting (surface dressing/tack), Medium-Setting (premix/patch), and Slow-Setting (prime coat/cold mix) emulsions.

qa.q: Why use emulsion instead of hot bitumen?
qa.a: It is safer, uses less fuel/energy, is eco-friendly and ideal for maintenance and rural works.
IS 8887 specifies bitumen emulsions in India.
RS for tack/surface dressing, MS for premix/patch repair, SS for prime coat and cold mixes.

qa.q: Is a plant expensive to set up?
qa.a: Emulsion plants are relatively accessible and modular; exact cost is sized in the DPR to your capacity.
Mainly a colloid mill with water/soap-phase and bitumen-phase tanks, dosing and storage.
PWD/rural-road contractors, municipalities, maintenance agencies and road developers.

qa.q: Can I make emulsion and modified bitumen together?
qa.a: Yes — many plants combine emulsion with PMB/CRMB lines for a broader product range.

qa.q: Do you offer turnkey emulsion plant consultancy?

products.title: Micro Surfacing Emulsion
products.subtitle: Polymer-modified emulsion for thin, fast-cure resurfacing (IRC SP:81)
products.titleTag: Micro Surfacing Emulsion Plant PMC, India | YUGA
products.metaDesc: Produce polymer-modified micro surfacing emulsion (IRC SP:81) for fast-cure resurfacing. YUGA delivers end-to-end plant PMC — DPR, licences & commissioning.
intro: Micro surfacing emulsion is a polymer-modified bitumen emulsion mixed with graded aggregate, mineral filler, water and additives to form a thin, durable surfacing layer applied cold by a specialised paver. It cures fast and reopens to traffic quickly.
It is a leading preventive-maintenance technique — sealing the surface, restoring skid resistance and correcting minor rutting without a full overlay. Guidance is given in IRC SP:81.
benefits: Fast traffic return — thin layer cures and opens within hours
Preventive maintenance — extends pavement life, delays costly overlay
Skid & ride quality — restores surface texture and seals cracks
Rut correction — can fill minor ruts in a single pass
Cold, green process — no hot-mix plant, lower energy and emissions
applications: Preventive maintenance of highways, airports and city roads
Skid-resistance restoration and surface sealing
Minor rut filling and ravelling correction
Heavily trafficked roads needing quick reopening

Lab for mix design & compatibility testing

qa.a: A thin, cold-applied layer of polymer-modified emulsion plus aggregate that seals and resurfaces roads quickly.

qa.q: How is it different from a normal emulsion?
qa.a: It uses polymer-modified emulsion and a designed aggregate mix laid by a special paver, for a structural thin surfacing — not just a coat.
It is preventive maintenance: extends pavement life, restores skid resistance and reopens to traffic in hours.
IRC SP:81 in India.
Minor ruts and ravelling, yes — often in a single pass; it is not a substitute for major structural repair.
Usually within a few hours, making it ideal for busy roads.
Polymer-modified bitumen emulsion, graded aggregate, mineral filler, water and additives.
Yes — a dedicated micro-surfacing paver/machine and a quality emulsion supply.
Highway authorities, airports, municipalities and maintenance contractors.

qa.q: Can you help me produce micro-surfacing emulsion?
qa.a: Yes — we cover feasibility, emulsion formulation, plant/equipment and marketing as part of our PMC service.

products.title: Blown (Oxidised) Bitumen
products.subtitle: Air-blown industrial-grade bitumen (IS 702) for waterproofing, roofing & industrial use
products.metaDesc: Set up a blown/oxidised bitumen plant (IS 702) for roofing, waterproofing & industrial use. YUGA's PMC covers DPR, licences, machinery & buyer network.
intro: Blown (oxidised) bitumen is made by blowing air through hot bitumen, which raises its softening point and lowers its penetration — giving a stiffer, more heat-resistant, less temperature-sensitive product. Grades are written as softening-point/penetration (e.g. 85/25, 90/15) under IS 702.

It is used mostly off-road — waterproofing, roofing felts, pipe coating, industrial flooring, sealants, adhesives and electrical compounds — which makes it a strong add-on line for an existing bitumen, PMB or CRMB plant.
benefits: Higher softening point — stays firm at high temperatures
Versatile industrial use — roofing, waterproofing, pipe coating, sealants
Add-on to existing plant — uses the same bitumen feed + an oxidation unit
Stable & long shelf life — less temperature-sensitive than paving grades
Standardised — grades per IS 702 (e.g. 85/25, 90/15, 115/15)
applications: Roofing felts and waterproofing membranes
Pipe coating and anti-corrosion lining
Industrial flooring, sealants and adhesives
Electrical/battery compounds and joint filling

details.label: Capacity to show
details.value: Small 2–5 TPH; Commercial 10–20 TPH (batch/continuous oxidation)

details.label: Area required
details.value: 10 TPH: 15,000–30,000 sq ft; with tank farm & loading 0.5–1 acre

details.label: Approx. cost (without land)
details.value: Oxidation unit add-on ₹0.5–2 Cr; standalone with tanks & safety ₹2–5 Cr

details.label: Utilities
details.value: Power, thermic/heating fuel, air blower/compressor, scrubber, fire water, lab

details.label: Approvals
details.value: SPCB CTE/CTO, factory/fire, emission control for blowing fumes, IS 702 testing

details.label: Best-use note
details.value: Best as an add-on to an existing bitumen/PMB/CRMB plant; steady industrial (non-road) demand

financials.note: Indicative for a blown-bitumen oxidation unit (often an add-on to an existing bitumen plant).

qa.a: Bitumen stiffened by blowing air through it, which raises softening point and lowers penetration for industrial (non-road) use.

qa.q: How is it different from paving bitumen?
qa.a: It is harder and more heat-resistant, and is used off-road — waterproofing, roofing, pipe coating — not for road surfacing.
Written as softening-point/penetration, e.g. 85/25, 90/15, 115/15, per IS 702.
Roofing felts, waterproofing, pipe coating, sealants, adhesives, industrial flooring and electrical compounds.
IS 702 for industrial blown-type bitumen in India.

qa.q: Can I make it alongside PMB/CRMB/emulsion?
qa.a: Yes — it uses the same bitumen feed plus an oxidation (air-blowing) unit, so it is a strong add-on product.
A blowing/oxidation column with heating, an air blower/compressor, fume condenser/scrubber and heated storage.
Waterproofing and roofing manufacturers, pipe-coating firms, industrial-product makers and traders.
It widens your product mix and serves steady industrial demand; exact economics are set in the DPR.

qa.q: Do you set up blown-bitumen units?
qa.a: Yes — feasibility, oxidation unit, safety/emission controls, IS-compliant grades and marketing are part of our PMC service.

products.title: Asphalt Roofing Shingle
products.subtitle: Fibreglass asphalt roofing shingles — a value-added, downstream product made from your own oxidised bitumen
products.titleTag: Asphalt Roofing Shingle Plant Setup | YUGA
products.metaDesc: Set up a fibreglass asphalt roofing-shingle plant with YUGA — a value-added line from oxidised bitumen. DPR, machinery, ASTM-grade output, buyers & licences.
intro: Asphalt shingles are the world's most common sloped-roof covering: a fibreglass mat is saturated and coated with oxidised (blown) bitumen, then surfaced with ceramic-coated mineral granules for weather, UV and fire resistance. They install fast, look premium and typically last 15–30 years.

For a bitumen or blown-bitumen operation, shingles are a natural value-added, downstream line — you convert your own oxidised bitumen into a finished consumer product at a far higher margin per tonne than selling bulk bitumen. India's demand is rising with villas, resorts and premium housing, and is still largely import-met — a genuine substitution opportunity.
benefits: Value-added — a finished roofing product at higher margin than bulk bitumen
Downstream fit — consumes your own oxidised / blown bitumen as feed
Rising demand — villas, resorts, farmhouses, prefab & premium housing
Import substitution — much of India's shingle demand is currently imported
Long product life — 15–30 yr weather, UV and (rated) fire resistance
applications: Sloped-roof residential — villas, bungalows, farmhouses
Resorts, clubhouses & hospitality architecture
Re-roofing & roof-repair market
Prefab / modular, gazebo and pergola structures

specs.label: Standards
specs.value: ASTM D3462 (fibreglass shingles); ASTM D3161 / D7158 (wind); UL 790 / ASTM E108 (fire)

specs.label: Composition
specs.value: Fibreglass mat + oxidised-bitumen coating + ceramic mineral granules

specs.label: Process
specs.value: Mat saturation & coating → granules → cooling → cutting → sealing → bundling

qa.a: A sloped-roof covering: a fibreglass mat coated with oxidised bitumen and surfaced with ceramic mineral granules for weather, UV and fire resistance.

qa.q: How does this fit a bitumen plant?
qa.a: It is a downstream, value-added line — you turn your own oxidised / blown bitumen into a finished roofing product at a higher margin than selling bulk bitumen.
Internationally, ASTM D3462 (fibreglass shingles), ASTM D3161 / D7158 (wind) and UL 790 / ASTM E108 (fire). India has no dedicated BIS shingle standard yet, so ASTM/UL norms are followed.
Oxidised / blown bitumen (your own feed), fibreglass mat, ceramic-coated mineral granules, limestone filler, sealant strip and release film.
Yes and growing — villas, resorts, farmhouses and premium housing — and much of it is currently imported, so domestic manufacturing is a substitution opportunity.

qa.q: Do you set up shingle plants?
qa.a: Yes — feasibility, line engineering, machinery sourcing, ASTM-grade QC and buyer development are part of our PMC service.

funding.intro: Who funds what: typical Indian bank/market norms. Promoter = your own money (margin); the rest is funded by a bank, NBFC, investor or the public. Figures are indicative — actual margin depends on the bank, your profile, collateral and scheme.

instruments.name: Term Loan (project finance)
instruments.note: PMEGP/CGTMSE can reduce your margin; CGTMSE covers collateral-free up to ₹5 Cr.
instruments.src: Banks fund 70–80% of project cost; promoter 20–30% margin (MSME term-loan margin 15–25%). Source: tatacapital.com, indifi.com, smeventure.com

instruments.name: Cash Credit (CC)
instruments.src: CC limit ≈ 75% of stock & book debts (25% margin). Source: neogrowth.in, famefintech.com

instruments.name: Overdraft (OD)
instruments.src: OD sanctioned against FD/property/collateral. Source: lendingkart.com, protium.co.in

instruments.name: Working Capital (Nayak / turnover method)
instruments.basis: Bank limit = 20% of projected annual turnover; you bring 5%
instruments.note: Standard MSME method up to ₹5 Cr.
instruments.src: Nayak Committee: WC limit = 20% of projected turnover; margin 5%. Source: neogrowth.in, tradecapital.co.in

instruments.name: Letter of Credit (LC)
instruments.forWhat: Buying raw material / imports on credit
instruments.note: Bank guarantees payment to your supplier.
instruments.src: LC margin typically 10–25% by creditworthiness. Source: hdfcbank.com, trade-finance norms

instruments.name: Seed / Startup Funding
instruments.funder: Angel / Startup India Seed Fund / incubator
instruments.note: Needs pitch deck, founder profile, market size.
instruments.src: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme. Source: seedfund.startupindia.gov.in

instruments.basis: Min promoter contribution 20% post-issue; EBITDA ≥ ₹1 Cr in 2 of last 3 yrs
instruments.note: Needs audited financials, governance, merchant banker (SEBI-registered).
instruments.src: SEBI SME-IPO 2025: EBITDA ≥ ₹1 Cr (2 of 3 yrs); promoter min 20% post-issue, lock-in. Source: SEBI; aocfo.com, indiaipo.in

market.note: National figures are from public industry/government sources (see source line). Reliable PRODUCT-WISE and STATE-WISE demand-vs-production-vs-import tonnage is NOT published openly for most of these products — so state demand is shown as DIRECTIONAL (high/medium) based on road activity & feedstock, not exact tonnage. Do not publish exact state tonnage without an official source.

qa.q: What exactly does a PMC do for me?
qa.a: We manage your entire project — feasibility, DPR, location, approvals, plant & machinery, finance/subsidy, commissioning, sales and growth — as one accountable partner, so you don't coordinate 40+ vendors, consultants and agencies yourself.

qa.q: Why hire YUGA instead of doing it myself?
qa.a: A plant means 25–60 machines, 10–40 suppliers, 10–18 agencies, 8–14 consultants and 18–25 departments. One mistake costs lakhs–crores. The time we save, errors we prevent and subsidies we capture usually exceed our fee.

qa.q: How is a PMC different from an EPC contractor?
qa.a: EPC builds for a fixed price but you lose flexibility and control. With a PMC, you keep choice and ownership while our experts manage every vendor and stage on your behalf.

qa.q: What is a DPR and why do I need it?
qa.a: A Detailed Project Report covers technical process, machinery, costs, revenue, DSCR, payback, risks and loan/subsidy assumptions — it is what banks and government schemes require to fund and approve your project.

qa.q: Can you help me choose the right product & location?
qa.a: Yes — our tools and team match the best product to your budget, state, feedstock and demand, and pick a site with the right feedstock proximity, logistics and state-policy advantage.

qa.q: How long does a plant take to set up?
qa.a: Typically several months to a year+ depending on product, capacity and approvals; a PMC compresses this by running stages in parallel and avoiding approval rejections.

qa.q: Do you handle approvals and compliance?
qa.a: Yes — SPCB CTE/CTO, CPCB/EPR, factory licence, fire NOC, PESO/boiler safety, GST/Udyam and product standards (BIS/IRC).
Engagement-based (feasibility, DPR, plant-setup PMC, retainer). We share a proposal after understanding your project — contact us for a quote.

qa.q: How much money do I need to bring myself?
qa.a: For a term loan, promoters typically bring 20–30% (margin); the bank funds 70–80%. Subsidies (e.g. PMEGP 15–35%) can reduce your share further. Use our Funding tool to see the split.

qa.q: Do you guarantee my loan or funding?
qa.a: No — funding depends on eligibility, documentation, lender/investor evaluation, due diligence and policy. We prepare the DPR/CMA, match the right instrument/scheme and liaise with banks/investors to maximise your chances.

qa.q: What funding options can YUGA help with?
qa.a: Term loan, cash credit (CC), overdraft (OD), letter of credit (LC), working capital, subsidy/grants, seed funding, angel/VC/PE, strategic JV and SME-IPO readiness.
A government scheme that lets banks give collateral-free loans up to ₹5 crore, covered by a credit guarantee — useful when you lack collateral.

qa.q: Am I eligible for an SME IPO?
qa.a: Broadly: 3-yr track record, EBITDA positive in 2 of last 3 years, post-issue capital within limits, and promoters hold min 20% (locked-in). We assess readiness and coordinate the merchant banker.
DPR, CMA data, project cost, promoter contribution, DSCR, collateral summary and a bank proposal — all of which we prepare.
No. We provide business documentation, financial modelling and fundraising-process support; securities-market advice requires SEBI-registered professionals.

qa.q: What does the YUGA software do for my business?
qa.a: It lets one person run a whole bitumen/petroleum business from one computer — manage thousands of old & new customers, talk to clients via AI CRM, broadcast to 2,400+ on WhatsApp/Telegram, get live market & logistics updates, and clean/verify data.

qa.q: Can the CRM really talk to my customers?
qa.a: Yes — the AI CRM can converse with customers, answer common queries, follow up and nudge plan upgrades automatically, with full chat history across WhatsApp & Telegram.

Quarterly or Yearly billing (yearly = best value), with four feature tiers A/B/C/D — you enable only the features you need and upgrade anytime. Indicative per-product plans are published in the pricing section.

qa.q: Can I message 2,400 customers at once?
qa.a: Yes — the petroleum/bitumen-base dashboard supports broadcasting to 2,400+ contacts at a time over WhatsApp & Telegram, segmented into old vs new buyers. (Bulk messaging must follow each platform's consent/anti-spam rules.)
An agentic desktop assistant that pushes live market prices (crude, USD-INR, bitumen) and logistics/dispatch updates so you act before competitors.

qa.q: What does the Excel filter & verification do?
qa.a: Upload any list — it filters full & new data, removes duplicates, standardises phone numbers, verifies contacts and exports a clean, CRM-ready, scored database.
Yes — customer data and financials are confidential with role-based access; bulk messaging follows platform consent and anti-spam policies.
Yes — book a demo and we'll show the dashboard, CRM and bots live, then suggest the right tier and billing for your team.

domestic: Technology / process tie-up with an Indian manufacturer
Contractor / offtake JV (assured buyer of output)
international: Foreign technology licensor (process know-how, e.g. CSIR/global)
International bitumen / crude supply tie-up (import security)
Foreign equity / FDI partner for capital + global standards
Export / offtake partner (sell output to overseas buyers)
Carbon-credit / ESG partner (e.g. global MRV platforms)
jv.note: JV needs a clear proposal, partner profile, business case, term sheet, roles & profit-sharing — all of which we prepare. International JV/FDI follows FEMA/RBI rules.
jv.src: Standard JV/FDI practice; FEMA/RBI for cross-border. Indicative.

pipeline: DPR / pitch deck / CMA / data room

loan.title: Loan Eligibility Self-Check
loan.note: Indicative only — final sanction depends on the bank's appraisal of viability, credit history, collateral & documents.

rules.name: Term Loan (Project Finance)
rules.say: You can apply — bring ~20–30% margin; bank funds 70–80%.

rules.name: PMEGP (new micro/small unit)
rules.say: New manufacturing unit — likely eligible for 15–35% capital subsidy.

rules.name: MUDRA Loan
rules.say: Small need ≤ ₹20 lakh — MUDRA route fits.

rules.name: Working Capital (CC/OD)
rules.say: Running business with turnover — CC/OD limit ≈ 20–25% of turnover.

rules.name: Letter of Credit (LC)
rules.say: You buy raw material/imports — LC fits (10–25% margin).

rules.name: Stand-Up India
rules.say: SC/ST/woman entrepreneur — Stand-Up India ₹10 lakh–₹1 Cr applies.

vc.title: VC / Equity Eligibility Self-Check
vc.note: Indicative — investors decide on team, market, traction & due diligence. Equity = giving ownership for capital.

rules.name: Govt Seed (Startup India)
rules.say: Early stage — Startup India Seed Fund (₹20–50 lakh) may fit (DPIIT recognition needed).

rules.name: Angel Investment
rules.say: Early traction + small need — angels (₹25 lakh–₹2 Cr) fit.

rules.name: Seed VC
rules.say: Some revenue + ₹2–10 Cr need — seed VC fits.

rules.name: Venture Debt
rules.say: Revenue exists — venture debt can extend runway with less dilution.

rules.name: Series A
rules.say: ₹1 Cr+ revenue + ₹10 Cr+ need — Series A range.

rules.name: Series B / C
rules.say: Scaling with ₹10 Cr+ revenue — Series B/C for expansion.

rules.name: Private Equity
rules.say: Mature, profitable scale — PE / pre-IPO capital.

ipo.title: IPO Readiness Self-Check (SME vs Main Board)
ipo.note: Indicative — final eligibility is decided by the exchange/SEBI with a merchant banker. Verify current ICDR norms.

rules.name: SME IPO (NSE Emerge / BSE SME)
rules.say: You likely meet SME-board basics: 3-yr track record, EBITDA positive (2 of 3 yrs), post-issue capital ≤ ₹25 Cr, promoter ≥ 20%.

rules.name: Main Board IPO (NSE / BSE)
rules.say: You may explore Main Board: strong profits (₹15 Cr+ avg) & net worth, 3-yr record, promoter ≥ 20%.

rules.name: Not yet — get IPO-ready
rules.say: Build 3-yr audited track record + EBITDA profit first; we prepare governance, financials & data room.
